bug_report.summary: YAML/frontmatter token styling/decoration changed unexpectedly (non-heading, non-code area)
steps_to_reproduce: Create or open a Markdown file that contains YAML frontmatter (e.g., `---`, `title: ...`, `keywords:` with list items).
View the file in the Markdown editor (as in the provided screenshots).
expected_behaviors: Frontmatter styling (token colors/decoration) should remain consistent with the pre-change behavior, since the refactor targets heading classes and code syntax highlighting only.
No new/missing/shifted decorations should appear within YAML/frontmatter unless explicitly intended.
actual_behaviors: Multiple token-level decoration differences appear in the YAML/frontmatter region (around `title`, `keywords`, and list items), indicating changes in syntax highlighting/decoration that are outside the stated heading/code-highlighting intent.

bug_report.summary: Bottom status/count area shows unexpected text rendering differences
steps_to_reproduce: Open a Markdown document in split view so the bottom status/count area is visible.
Observe the bottom bar near the word/character counts and rendering status.
expected_behaviors: Status/count text should render identically before vs. after the refactor (no visual change), since the change intent does not mention status bar rendering.
actual_behaviors: Small visual differences are detected in the bottom status/count region (word/character count area), suggesting unintended rendering/layout changes.

bug_report.summary: Rendered preview inline-code/keycap styling changed (e.g., “Command” and “Control” badges)
steps_to_reproduce: Open a document in split view where the rendered preview includes inline-code/keycap-like elements (e.g., the “Command” and “Control” badges shown).
Compare the appearance of these inline-code elements before vs after the change.
expected_behaviors: Preview inline-code rendering should remain unchanged by an editor-side CodeMirror decoration refactor.
actual_behaviors: Inline-code/keycap elements in the preview render with different styling in the after state (detected at the “Command” and “Control” badges), suggesting unintended CSS/styling changes affecting the preview.

bug_report.summary: Fenced JSON code block syntax highlighting appearance changed (token colors/decoration differ)
steps_to_reproduce: Create/open a Markdown file.
Insert a fenced JSON code block, e.g.:
```json
"marker": "UNIQUE_TOKEN_TOP_4F2E",
"value": 123
expected_behaviors: JSON code block syntax highlighting should look the same as before the refactor (same coloring/decoration for punctuation, keys, strings, and numbers).
actual_behaviors: Token-level highlighting within the JSON code block differs after the change (multiple diffs across the language tag and JSON tokens; Parsed_Info no. 9–16).

bug_report.summary: Fenced Bash code block syntax highlighting appearance changed (token colors/decoration differ)
steps_to_reproduce: Create/open a Markdown file.
Insert a fenced Bash code block, e.g.:
```bash
# marker below must be findable
export TOKEN="UNIQUE_TOKEN_BOTTOM_9C7A"
echo "$TOKEN"
expected_behaviors: Bash code block syntax highlighting should match the pre-change appearance (same token colors/decoration for comments, keywords/commands, strings, variables, and punctuation).
actual_behaviors: Token-level highlighting within the Bash code block differs after the change (multiple diffs across the code-fence area and code tokens; see Parsed_Info no. 9–18, 20–27).

bug_report.summary: Unexpected floating formatting toolbar appears after pressing Enter in Find-in-file to jump to match
steps_to_reproduce: Open a Markdown file with content (including a fenced code block).
Press Ctrl+F to open Find-in-file.
Enter a search term that has a match in the document (e.g., UNIQUE_TOKEN_BOTTOM_9C7A).
Press Enter to jump to the match.
expected_behaviors: The editor should jump to the match and highlight it without showing any additional floating formatting/quick-action toolbar overlays.
actual_behaviors: A floating formatting/quick-action toolbar (with icons such as bold/italic etc.) appears over the bottom-right of the editor pane after the jump (visible only in the after-change screenshot; diff region aligns with Parsed_Info no. 18–19).

bug_report.summary: Preview-rendered code/frontmatter snippet styling differs (token/background placement changes)
steps_to_reproduce: Open the “Welcome to Zettlr!” document in Preview (center pane).
Scroll to/locate the small code/frontmatter-like snippet near the top of the document (the multi-line block with colored tokens).
Compare token/background placement and styling between builds.
expected_behaviors: Preview code block rendering/styling should not change as a result of moving editor (CodeMirror) syntax highlighting/decorations to viewport-only processing.
actual_behaviors: The snippet’s rendered token/background/decoration appearance differs between builds (diff regions no. 2 through no. 10).

bug_report.summary: Undo (Ctrl+Z) has no visible effect on Markdown content in the editor pane (table remains unchanged)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown file.
Enable Preview mode / split view as in the scenario.
Type a Markdown table (e.g., `| Col1 | Col2 |` + separator + data row).
Press Ctrl+Z (Undo) while focus is in the editor pane.
expected_behaviors: The most recent text entry should be undone (table content should visibly change/reduce with each Ctrl+Z, eventually reaching an empty document after repeated Undo).
actual_behaviors: After Ctrl+Z, the table content remains visually unchanged (no visible removal of table rows/text).
